I don't know.

channel, but I'm going to go and share my screen as well.

So we published two articles.

The new one, this one, the top AI use cases and financial services, it's already been indexed by Google.

That means that Google's crawlers, like, discovered it automatically and indexed it, so it can already start appearing in search results, which is great, because Google's crawlers and generally search engines, they don't want to serve content that's thin and unhelpful.
So by being indexed, it means that the content is comprehensive enough.
So

So, yeah, we're going to add this URL and the subsequent URLs that we publish, we're going to add them to our Looker dashboard so we can start tracking the performance of these blog posts that we've been publishing.
And then the next step is to publish last week's batch of content.
Is there a separate Looker dashboard you guys are creating for us?
There is a Looker dashboard, there's a report in the dashboard called Cohorts, and it's going to just focus on GrowthX content performance.
I can share it right now.
Yeah, yeah, I would love that.
don't know if we have.

Let me just check.
We have it set up for you.

So, yeah, it's a multi-page report.
And the content performance of GrowthX content is going to be here in cohorts.
So, this page filters non-GrowthX content performance from GrowthX.
So, and it pulls data from both, like, Google Analytics 4, which is this table right here.
So, you can see the engagement rate.
And then it pulls data from Google Search Console, which is just Google Search traffic.
So, we can see the impact on just, like, Google Search and Google, like, overall, like, performance.
And then for, I mean, these two tables.
The way that they display the traffic is that they categorize the content into the topic clusters that we have, like, right here.
these are the topic clusters that we're targeting with our initial strategy.
Alexis, you're not able to.
I'll share access with you.

report.

And then you can also see the landing pages that are getting the most traffic from LLMs.

was trying my personal, which was weird because I'm logged in with my work email.

Yeah.
Yeah.
So, Um...
All right.
And then you can also, for this report, it's super important to kind of see the engagement rate.
I think I broke this down when I was first presenting the performance report, but basically the engagement rate is the opposite of bounce rate.
So an engaged session is a session that lasts longer than 10 seconds, or it includes a page scroll or includes somebody clicking on a link on your site.
So what's really interesting is that for a lot of these landing pages that are getting clicks from LLMs, your engagement rate is quite high.
So over 50% for like the vast majority of these, of these URLs.
And what's interesting is that when you go to your like overall like sessions versus engaged sessions, is that overall your engagement rate is quite low compared to the totality of your traffic.

So that's something to kind of explore in the sense that we want to be able to see like, okay, what are the traffic, like what are the, at least when we're publishing our blog content, we want to be able to see like what blog posts get more engagement versus less engagement and how can we optimize what's not performing well to get more engagement.
And this typically means just making the content easier to like more readable.
So like making the sections, like, you know, chunking them and like making it very clear what each section is going to cover.
But yeah, so that's kind of an interesting thing that I wanted to flag to you.
All right, and then if you have any requests for how you want us to customize this performance report, let us know and I'll check in with our team to see what we can do.
We have been able to like create additional reports or like additional views to.
So, like, just, like, provide the insights that you're really looking for more easily.
All right.
And then for the content that we are working on this week, so the five new articles for this week are already ready for review.
I'll send them in the external channel and tag both Cody and Allison.
I know that Allison reviewed last week's batch of content.
These are five blogs.

Because his feedback so far, I believe, hasn't been very extensive.

see what the batch?

correct.

Cool.

I know that, so, Cody let us know last night that we do need thumbnail images for all of these blogs.

would be, I think.

Canva.

Yes, I could do that right now.

Yeah, that definitely works.

So yeah, we'll set that up for you ASAP, and then we can like go back and like regenerate images for every single one of the blog posts that we've already delivered so that it's very quick to publish.
Okay, great.
and then for...
Okay,
So, the article that we refreshed, which is, what is an AI agent?
I was wondering if it's possible to change the date so that it's updated.
Okay.

Okay.
Perfect.
All right.
And then, yeah.
So, I am going to, right now, I am working on a summary of just phase one.

We're also working on a technical SEO audit for you.
So, it's going to just include a list of any potential issues that we've found on your site that should be resolved to just boost your overall SEO and just, like, website performance.
And, yeah.
That's kind of the week seven, like, checkpoint that we're at right now.
Yeah, I think so far we're really happy with it.
Obviously, it's a lot of the hard part is, like, us reviewing the content, but we're hoping that's a short-term pain for our end.
We don't use Airtable internally, so it's really hard for us to go between, like, Airtable and Notion because we don't use either.
Right.
So, but again, I think for us, I'm hoping this is more like a short-term pain since the goal eventually is, like, we won't, we can just, we just have to focus more on the reporting side and outcome.
Yeah, exactly.
Now, the, what are you using internally?
We use Asana, and then we use Asana, and then, Asana for, yeah, Asana Asana, for Airtable, and then for Notion, we use Google Docs.
Yeah, got it.
Got it.
Yeah, I think with, um...
So where we're at right now is that the reviews during the sprint, we do more detailed reviews because that just allows us to catch any potential issues.
And then once we're really out of the sprint, then we could get to publishing very consistently and the reviews don't have to be extensive.
You can just kind of give it like a quick look before it's published on your site.

And I mean, the team that's been like generating your content, they've really been like taking into account all of the feedback and like just constantly like improving the article generation pipeline.
So I think, you know, our goal really is to kind of like make sure you're not spending a ton of time doing these reviews.
And my understanding is that the feedback from Allison and Cody hasn't been like super, super extensive.
So I'm like just happy to know that like they don't have to spend a lot of time just reviewing this.
Yeah, same.
I think for us, it's just like, obviously right now for us, it's just like using a different tool and getting your views in.

But again, I think this is, for us, like we're hoping once, because our PMM is pretty tight on resources, so that's always been hard for getting the, and that was our struggle when we had an SEO agency was really like the internal PMM review side.

But when I spoke with Cody, I think the group, we both had an understanding that this is more just like an eight-week sprint, like an eight-week sprint, and then we're hoping that PMM can be removed from the process.
Yeah, absolutely.
So yeah, I think we're happy.
So I think for answers, we're happy with this partnership so far.
Cool.
I'm happy to hear that.

Lynn, I wonder if there are some, if some of these assets who are like deeper technical, I don't know how technical we are going to get in these, but if it is, like I wonder if we can, if PMMs are busy, then we can ask like somebody from the essay team to just give a look to make sure.
It's technically accurate, you know?
Yeah, for sure.

think we've also, Cody's also been handing some of them off to the technical marketing team, but the original, like, I think when Megan's original goal was because the goal is to launch up to 40 a quarter, it's impossible internally to scale at that level.
So I think once we feel good, generally speaking, about that technical accuracy in the first two months, we'd want to be as hands-off.
But yeah, I think if, unless it's, like, super technical, then we would definitely get someone involved to review.
Yeah.
Okay.
Okay.
Perfect.
I don't, I think those are all of the updates that we had for you.
Erik, was there anything else on your end?
No, I think from a production standpoint, that covers everything.
I think just for, I know we pinged about it in the channel, but for phase two, we just need kind of a...
Verbal or written agreement that you guys are kind of in line with going forward with us.
We do have this eight-week period of kind of, we call it dating, making sure that you guys are happy with the production and making sure that kind of the workflow works for you.
So I think as long as everything looks good there, we'll just need kind of confirmation on moving forward to Phase 2.
Sorry, I'm slacking you guys right now with the written verbal confirmation.
Wonderful.
Cool.
Okay, I just left it, so we're good to go.
Okay, thank you.
All right, and I just wanted to, oh, hi, Tyler.
No, hey there.
Just wanted to say hello to the team, and Reet, nice to meet you.
I'm on, you know, the sales team at GrowthX, so if you have any questions about the commercials or, you know, what you get out of the partnership, just let us know.
But I'll stay out of the team's way here and just an extra resource if you need me.
All right.
All All
Awesome.
All right.
Anything else, Team, that you need help with or wanted to discuss?
I don't think so.
No?
All right.
Yeah.
I just, before we jump, I wanted to kind of show you just one thing that the team has been kind of experimenting with for your content.
Because, you know, from our conversation with Allison, like, we know that it's, like, really important to kind of hit the right tone.
And in our artifacts, we, this is like your workspace.
And we created, like, a custom artifact that's called Examples of Good.
And it's just two articles that capture your tone and are good examples of the kind of content that we want to emulate.
And it's just, like, the articles that you, like, you know, that hit the mark with Allison.
And then during the content generation process, towards...
Once the article is drafted, AI can compare the draft to these good examples and grade the draft and find areas for improvement.

And this is just one example of a custom artifact that we can generate for you, but anything that comes from the PMM team, it's just important that any feedback from them is communicated to us so that we can build a custom artifact here for just any repetitive feedback that they have for us or anything that's super important for us to bring up in content.
There really is no, there's nothing that, I feel like it's too custom here in terms of just customizing the workflow to make sure that the articles look the way you want them to look.
And we're quite flexible in building these and adding them on an ad hoc basis.
Got it.
How, what's the best way for our PMMs or technical marketers to share?
Share artifacts?
Should I do it through Slack?
Should I do it through?
Typically, we generate the artifacts.
However, anything that they want to share with us, they can just Slack us.
We'll just give you any docs as we have updated information.
Okay.
Yeah, exactly.
Yeah.
And then we also have in the workflow projects, we have the agentic article generation here.
And then we can, like, add, like, we have already have the workflow for generating cover images, but we just need to update the backend so that it looks like your Canva images.
So that's what we're going to do.
Okay.
Okay.
Awesome.

Sounds good.

Thank you.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Sync on content production, review performance, and confirm Phase 2.](https://fathom.video/share/PUNAVufGwBX3urVT3JAzJES2vtt8Trzg?tab=summary&timestamp=237.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Content Live:** Two articles are live; "Top AI Use Cases in Financial Services" is already indexed by Google, signaling quality.](https://fathom.video/share/PUNAVufGwBX3urVT3JAzJES2vtt8Trzg?tab=summary&timestamp=244.0)
  - [**Performance Tracking:** A new Looker dashboard tracks GrowthX content performance by topic cluster and LLM referral, revealing high engagement (\>50%) from LLM traffic.](https://fathom.video/share/PUNAVufGwBX3urVT3JAzJES2vtt8Trzg?tab=summary&timestamp=323.0)
  - [**Workflow Streamlined:** GrowthX will now create blog thumbnails using Redis's Canva templates, removing a bottleneck for the Redis team.](https://fathom.video/share/PUNAVufGwBX3urVT3JAzJES2vtt8Trzg?tab=summary&timestamp=933.0)
  - [**Phase 2 Approved:** Fung-Lin confirmed the partnership will continue, moving past the initial 8-week trial.](https://fathom.video/share/PUNAVufGwBX3urVT3JAzJES2vtt8Trzg?tab=summary&timestamp=1372.0)

## Topics

### Content Production & Review

  - [**Live Content:** Two articles are published.](https://fathom.video/share/PUNAVufGwBX3urVT3JAzJES2vtt8Trzg?tab=summary&timestamp=244.0)
      - ["Top AI Use Cases in Financial Services" is already indexed by Google, confirming its quality and comprehensiveness.](https://fathom.video/share/PUNAVufGwBX3urVT3JAzJES2vtt8Trzg?tab=summary&timestamp=268.0)
  - [**Backlog:** Last week's batch of 5 articles is ready for review.](https://fathom.video/share/PUNAVufGwBX3urVT3JAzJES2vtt8Trzg?tab=summary&timestamp=730.0)
      - [**Status:** 3 articles are ready to publish; 2 require minor edits based on Allison's feedback.](https://fathom.video/share/PUNAVufGwBX3urVT3JAzJES2vtt8Trzg?tab=summary&timestamp=791.0)
      - [**Process:** Fung-Lin will coordinate with the PMM team for a final review.](https://fathom.video/share/PUNAVufGwBX3urVT3JAzJES2vtt8Trzg?tab=summary&timestamp=832.0)
  - [**Future State:** The goal is to reduce PMM review time after the initial 8-week sprint to enable scaling to 40 articles/quarter.](https://fathom.video/share/PUNAVufGwBX3urVT3JAzJES2vtt8Trzg?tab=summary&timestamp=1266.0)
      - [GrowthX will build a "Examples of Good" artifact from approved articles to train its AI on Redis's tone, improving draft quality.](https://fathom.video/share/PUNAVufGwBX3urVT3JAzJES2vtt8Trzg?tab=summary&timestamp=1456.0)

### Performance Tracking (Looker Dashboard)

  - [Aida demoed the new Looker dashboard for tracking GrowthX content performance.](https://fathom.video/share/PUNAVufGwBX3urVT3JAzJES2vtt8Trzg?tab=summary&timestamp=323.0)
  - [**Key Features:**](https://fathom.video/share/PUNAVufGwBX3urVT3JAzJES2vtt8Trzg?tab=summary&timestamp=365.0)
      - [**Cohorts Page:** Filters data to show only GrowthX content, broken down by topic cluster.](https://fathom.video/share/PUNAVufGwBX3urVT3JAzJES2vtt8Trzg?tab=summary&timestamp=377.0)
      - [**LLM Referral Report:** Shows traffic from Large Language Models (LLMs).](https://fathom.video/share/PUNAVufGwBX3urVT3JAzJES2vtt8Trzg?tab=summary&timestamp=471.0)
          - [**Top Sources:** ChatGPT, Perplexity, Google Gemini.](https://fathom.video/share/PUNAVufGwBX3urVT3JAzJES2vtt8Trzg?tab=summary&timestamp=493.0)
          - [**Insight:** LLM-referred traffic has a high engagement rate (\>50%), indicating strong content-audience fit.](https://fathom.video/share/PUNAVufGwBX3urVT3JAzJES2vtt8Trzg?tab=summary&timestamp=601.0)
  - [**Action:** Aida will add a table to track individual article performance.](https://fathom.video/share/PUNAVufGwBX3urVT3JAzJES2vtt8Trzg?tab=summary&timestamp=455.0)

### Blog Thumbnails

  - [**Requirement:** Thumbnails are needed for all blog posts for social sharing and the main blog page.](https://fathom.video/share/PUNAVufGwBX3urVT3JAzJES2vtt8Trzg?tab=summary&timestamp=933.0)
  - [**Solution:** GrowthX will create them.](https://fathom.video/share/PUNAVufGwBX3urVT3JAzJES2vtt8Trzg?tab=summary&timestamp=933.0)
      - [**Process:** Alexis will provide screenshots of Redis's Canva templates (including color palettes). GrowthX's designer will then replicate the style.](https://fathom.video/share/PUNAVufGwBX3urVT3JAzJES2vtt8Trzg?tab=summary&timestamp=987.0)
      - [**Action:** GrowthX will generate thumbnails for all previously delivered articles.](https://fathom.video/share/PUNAVufGwBX3urVT3JAzJES2vtt8Trzg?tab=summary&timestamp=1060.0)

### Phase 2 & Future Initiatives

  - [**Phase 2 Confirmation:** Fung-Lin confirmed the partnership will continue beyond the 8-week trial.](https://fathom.video/share/PUNAVufGwBX3urVT3JAzJES2vtt8Trzg?tab=summary&timestamp=1372.0)
  - [**Upcoming Deliverables:**](https://fathom.video/share/PUNAVufGwBX3urVT3JAzJES2vtt8Trzg?tab=summary&timestamp=1110.0)
      - [**Phase 1 Summary:** A report on progress and plans for Phase 2.](https://fathom.video/share/PUNAVufGwBX3urVT3JAzJES2vtt8Trzg?tab=summary&timestamp=1110.0)
      - [**Technical SEO Audit:** A list of site issues to resolve for improved SEO and performance.](https://fathom.video/share/PUNAVufGwBX3urVT3JAzJES2vtt8Trzg?tab=summary&timestamp=1122.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Aida:**](https://fathom.video/share/PUNAVufGwBX3urVT3JAzJES2vtt8Trzg?tab=summary&timestamp=300.0)
      - [Add new article URLs to the Looker dashboard.](https://fathom.video/share/PUNAVufGwBX3urVT3JAzJES2vtt8Trzg?tab=summary&timestamp=300.0)
      - [Add a table for individual article performance to the dashboard.](https://fathom.video/share/PUNAVufGwBX3urVT3JAzJES2vtt8Trzg?tab=summary&timestamp=455.0)
      - [Share the "Examples of Good" artifact with the team.](https://fathom.video/share/PUNAVufGwBX3urVT3JAzJES2vtt8Trzg?tab=summary&timestamp=1456.0)
  - [**Alexis:**](https://fathom.video/share/PUNAVufGwBX3urVT3JAzJES2vtt8Trzg?tab=summary&timestamp=1038.0)
      - [Send screenshots of Canva thumbnail templates (with color palettes) to Aida.](https://fathom.video/share/PUNAVufGwBX3urVT3JAzJES2vtt8Trzg?tab=summary&timestamp=1038.0)
  - [**Fung-Lin:**](https://fathom.video/share/PUNAVufGwBX3urVT3JAzJES2vtt8Trzg?tab=summary&timestamp=832.0)
      - [Coordinate PMM review for the 5-article batch.](https://fathom.video/share/PUNAVufGwBX3urVT3JAzJES2vtt8Trzg?tab=summary&timestamp=832.0)
  - [**GrowthX Team:**](https://fathom.video/share/PUNAVufGwBX3urVT3JAzJES2vtt8Trzg?tab=summary&timestamp=933.0)
      - [Generate thumbnails for all previously delivered articles.](https://fathom.video/share/PUNAVufGwBX3urVT3JAzJES2vtt8Trzg?tab=summary&timestamp=1060.0)
      - [Update the "What is an AI Agent?" article's publish date.](https://fathom.video/share/PUNAVufGwBX3urVT3JAzJES2vtt8Trzg?tab=summary&timestamp=1080.0)

action_items.description: Add URLs of published Redis blog posts to GrowthX Looker dashboard
Send this week's 5 articles to Cody/Allison in Slack; then publish on approval
Resolve Allison's comments on 'Vector Similarity' and 'Database Performance Optimization'
Ping PMM re: last week's batch; then send green light to Aida
Send Fung-Lin Slack thread link for last week's batch
Send Aida thumbnail screenshots/templates; then GrowthX implement generator + backfill
Update 'What is an AI agent?' blog post date in CMS
